findings.title: Search Relevance Score Drop
findings.description: The recent deployment (v2.4.1) has caused a significant drop in search relevance scores, which could lead to user dissatisfaction and decreased engagement. Immediate action is required to analyze and remediate this issue.
assumptions: The drop in relevance is directly linked to the new ranking algorithm introduced in v2.4.1.

evidence.excerpt: Our p50 search relevance score fell from 0.85 to 0.72 immediately following the v2.4.1 release.

recommendations: Conduct a root-cause analysis of the ranking algorithm.
Consider a rollback if remediation is not feasible.

findings.title: Search Latency Increase on Mobile
findings.description: The increase in search latency on mobile web indicates a potential issue with the new ranking model's performance. This requires profiling to identify the cause and potential optimizations.
assumptions: The new ranking model is causing increased latency due to a heavier re-ranking step.

evidence.excerpt: P95 search latency on mobile web has climbed to 890ms, up from a baseline of ~420ms pre-deploy.

recommendations: Profile the new ranking model to identify performance bottlenecks.

findings.title: Missing Search Analytics Events
findings.description: The absence of analytics events for new query types (image and voice search) limits visibility into their performance and user engagement. Instrumentation must be added to track these events.
assumptions: Instrumentation for new query types was not included in the v2.4.1 release.

evidence.excerpt: Events 'image_search_query', 'voice_search_query', and 'filter_applied' are completely absent from our data pipeline.

recommendations: Add baseline instrumentation for image and voice search events.

findings.title: Inconclusive A/B Test Results
findings.description: The inconclusive results from the A/B test for the new ranking algorithm indicate a risk in decision-making based on incomplete data. This could lead to further degradation of search relevance.
assumptions: The A/B test did not adequately capture the performance of the new ranking algorithm for anonymous users.

evidence.excerpt: Results were inconclusive: relevance improved +2% for signed-in users but degraded -8% for anonymous users.

recommendations: Reassess the A/B testing strategy to ensure comprehensive coverage of user segments.

findings.title: Baseline Metrics for Image Search
findings.description: The lack of baseline metrics for image search hampers the ability to measure its success and make informed decisions for future improvements. Establishing these metrics is critical.
assumptions: Baseline metrics were not established prior to the launch of image search.

evidence.excerpt: We need to establish: query volume, zero-result rate, click-through rate on image results.

recommendations: Define and implement a strategy to gather baseline metrics for image search.

findings.title: Stale Autocomplete Suggestions
findings.description: The extension of the cache TTL for autocomplete suggestions has led to users encountering stale data, compounding the relevance issue. This needs to be addressed to improve user experience.
assumptions: The cache TTL was inadvertently extended during the deployment.

evidence.excerpt: Since v2.4.1 the cache TTL was inadvertently extended to 7 days.

recommendations: Revert the cache TTL for autocomplete suggestions to a more appropriate duration.

summary: The analysis reveals critical risks related to search relevance and performance following the v2.4.1 deployment. Key dependencies include the need for analytics instrumentation and baseline metrics for new features. Immediate remediation actions are recommended to address the drop in relevance scores and increased latency, while a phased delivery plan is proposed to prioritize MVP features and subsequent enhancements.
